steps.title: Write both surface areas
steps.content:
Let $s$ be the side length of a square base. One prism has height $80$, so
$$K=2s^2+4s(80)=2s^2+320s$$
After the prisms are glued along a square base, the new prism has height $160$, so its surface area is
$$2s^2+4s(160)=2s^2+640s$$

steps.title: Use the given ratio
steps.content:
The combined surface area is $\frac{33}{17}K$. Set up the ratio and cancel the common factor $2s$.
$$\frac{2s^2+640s}{2s^2+320s}=\frac{33}{17}$$
$$\frac{s+320}{s+160}=\frac{33}{17}$$
